chain.thought:
Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
deaths -> deaths per year
column value of the question link to columns:

semantic sentence of the question link to columns:

The question is asking to determine which period had the same number of deaths as the previous year had. Since we need to compare the deaths per year values between consecutive periods, we should sort the data chronologically using the "period" column with the order "small to large" (from earliest to latest) using the f_sort_column(period) operation. This will allow us to compare the deaths per year values between consecutive periods.

The question is asking to identify the period where the number of deaths per year remained the same as the previous period. Since each row represents a time period and the "deaths per year" column indicates the mortality rate for that period, we need to compare the values in the "deaths per year" column between consecutive periods. We can use the f_group_column(deaths per year) operation to analyze any matching values in adjacent rows.

The 1995-2000 and 2000-2005 periods both had 194,000 deaths per year, which means the 2000-2005 period had the same number of deaths as the previous period (1995-2000). Similarly, the 2005-2010 period had slightly more deaths (196,000) than the previous period (194,000), so it doesn't qualify. Therefore, the period that had the same number of deaths as the previous year is 2000-2005.

judge_thought:
Explanation: The question asks which period had the same number of deaths as the previous year had. To determine this, we need to look at the "deaths per year" column and compare each period with the one preceding it. From the original table:

- 1950-1955: 66,000
- 1955-1960: 76,000 (increase from 66,000)
- 1960-1965: 89,000 (increase from 76,000)
- 1965-1970: 105,000 (increase from 89,000)
- 1970-1975: 121,000 (increase from 105,000)
- 1975-1980: 138,000 (increase from 121,000)
- 1980-1985: 157,000 (increase from 138,000)
- 1985-1990: 179,000 (increase from 157,000)
- 1990-1995: 192,000 (increase from 179,000)
- 1995-2000: 194,000 (increase from 192,000)
- 2000-2005: 194,000 (same as 1995-2000)
- 2005-2010: 196,000 (increase from 194,000)

The only period where the number of deaths remained the same as the previous period is **2000-2005**, matching the Prediction Answer.
